title: Witch Week - Chapter 14 (Fragment 4)
text:
‚ÄúCan you see anything Brian forgot?‚Äù asked the dim shape of Chrestomanci.

Nan began to laugh. ‚ÄúI didn‚Äôt say the carpet or the petrol!‚Äù

‚ÄúBrian suggested a carpet. And I thought that corner looked a little bare,‚Äù Chrestomanci admitted.

Nan pointed to the pile of black books. ‚ÄúWhat are those?‚Äù

‚ÄúDisguised schedules,‚Äù said Chrestomanci. ‚ÄúOh‚ÄîI see what you mean. Obviously they are Acts of Parliament and Witchcraft Edicts, torture manuals, and The Observer‚Äôs Guide to Witch-Spotting. No inquisitor would be without them.‚Äù

Nan could tell from his voice that he was laughing. ‚ÄúI accuse you of enjoying yourself,‚Äù she said, ‚Äúwhile everyone outside gibbers.‚Äù

‚ÄúI confess to that.‚Äù Chrestomanci came around the desk under the light. He pushed the spitting bunch of wires casually aside‚Äîit did not appear to give him any kind of shock‚Äîand sat on the black-draped desk so that his face was level with Nan‚Äôs. She suddenly found it almost impossible to look away. ‚ÄúI accuse you of enjoying yourself too,‚Äù he said.

‚ÄúYes, I have!‚Äù Nan said defiantly. ‚ÄúFor about the first time since I came to this beastly school!‚Äù

Chrestomanci looked at her almost anxiously. ‚ÄúYou enjoy being a witch?‚Äù Nan nodded vigorously. ‚ÄúAnd you‚Äôve enjoyed making things up and describing them‚Äîthumbscrews and so on?‚Äù Nan nodded again. ‚ÄúWhich did you enjoy most?‚Äù Chrestomanci asked.

‚ÄúOh, being a witch,‚Äù Nan declared. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs made me feel‚Äîwell‚Äîjust so confident, I suppose.‚Äù

‚ÄúDescribe the things you‚Äôve invented so far to do as a witch,‚Äù said Chrestomanci.

‚ÄúI‚Äî‚Äù Nan looked at Chrestomanci, lit from one side by the strong light of the lamp and, from the other, by the flickering wires, and was rather puzzled to find how little she had done as a witch. All she had done, when you came down to it, was to ride a broomstick and to give herself and Estelle the wrong kind of clothes and some decidedly odd collecting tins. ‚ÄúI haven‚Äôt had much time to do things yet,‚Äù she said.

‚ÄúBut Charles Morgan has had about the same amount of time, and according to the things people have been telling me, he has been very inventive indeed,‚Äù Chrestomanci said. ‚ÄúWouldn‚Äôt you say that, now that you‚Äôve been a witch, and got your confidence, you might really prefer describing things even to witchcraft?‚Äù

Nan thought about it. ‚ÄúI suppose I would,‚Äù she said, rather surprised. ‚ÄúIf only we didn‚Äôt have to do it in our journals!‚Äù

‚ÄúGood,‚Äù said Chrestomanci. ‚ÄúI think I can promise you one really good opportunity to describe things, nothing to do with journals. I told you it would take strong magic to put this world back into the one where it belongs. When I find the way to do it, I shall need everyone‚Äôs help, in order to harness all the magic there is in the world to make the change. When the time comes, can I rely on you to explain all this?‚Äù

Nan nodded. She felt hugely flattered and responsible.

While she was feeling this way, Chrestomanci added, ‚ÄúJust as well you prefer describing things. I‚Äôm afraid you won‚Äôt be a witch when the change comes.‚Äù Nan stared at him. He was not joking. ‚ÄúI know you are descended from the Archwitch,‚Äù Chrestomanci said, ‚Äúbut talents don‚Äôt always descend in the same shape. Yours seem to have come to you in the form of making-up and describing. My advice is to stick with that, if you can. Now name me one character out of history.‚Äù

vocabulary.example: ‚ÄúI accuse you of enjoying yourself,‚Äù she said, ‚Äúwhile everyone outside gibbers.

‚Äù

Chrestomanci looked at her almost anxiously.

vocabulary.definition: In an unconcerned or informal manner.
vocabulary.example: He pushed the spitting bunch of wires casually aside‚Äîit did not appear to give him any kind of shock‚Äîand sat on the black-draped desk so that his face was level with Nan‚Äôs.

vocabulary.definition: A proper noun, name of a character, used lexically in the text.
vocabulary.example: ‚Äù asked the dim shape of Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling or showing certainty about something.
vocabulary.example: ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs made me feel‚Äîwell‚Äîjust so confident, I suppose.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner showing resistance or bold disobedience.

vocabulary.example: ‚Äù asked the dim shape of Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Having the appearance changed to conceal identity.
An official order or proclamation issued by authority.
vocabulary.example: Obviously they are Acts of Parliament and Witchcraft Edicts, torture manuals, and The Observer‚Äôs Guide to Witch-Spotting.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling pleased because of praise or attention.
vocabulary.example: She felt hugely flattered and responsible.

vocabulary.definition: To control and make use of (natural resources, energy, etc.).
vocabulary.example: When I find the way to do it, I shall need everyone‚Äôs help, in order to harness all the magic there is in the world to make the change.

vocabulary.definition: A person who investigates and questions thoroughly, especially in historical contexts of religious tribunals.
vocabulary.example: No inquisitor would be without them.

vocabulary.definition: Having the ability to create or design new things.
vocabulary.example: ‚ÄúBut Charles Morgan has had about the same amount of time, and according to the things people have been telling me, he has been very inventive indeed,‚Äù Chrestomanci said.

vocabulary.definition: The highest legislative body in a country.
vocabulary.example: Obviously they are Acts of Parliament and Witchcraft Edicts, torture manuals, and The Observer‚Äôs Guide to Witch-Spotting.

vocabulary.definition: Having the duty to deal with something or control someone.
vocabulary.example: She felt hugely flattered and responsible.

vocabulary.definition: A plan of activities or events.
Infliction of severe pain or suffering for punishment or coercion.
vocabulary.example: Obviously they are Acts of Parliament and Witchcraft Edicts, torture manuals, and The Observer‚Äôs Guide to Witch-Spotting.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows strong, healthy energy.
